title: Intentional Acetaminophen Overdose ~25 g + Concomitant Alcohol 12 Hours Ago in 38-Year-Old Woman with Major Depressive Disorder Post-Relationship Conflict: 21-Hour IV N-Acetylcysteine Protocol, Rumack-Matthew Nomogram Interpretation, King's College Criteria for Liver Transplant Referral, Psychiatric Hold + Safety Planning + Capacity Assessment, and Family-Centered Communication
subtitle: ACETAMINOPHEN OVERDOSE = leading cause of acute liver failure in US (~50% of cases — 40% intentional + 60% unintentional/staggered); TOXIC DOSE >150 mg/kg single ingestion or >7.5 g in adult (LOWER threshold with chronic alcohol + malnutrition + fasting + isoniazid + CYP2E1-inducing drugs); 4 CLINICAL STAGES = Stage I 0–24 hr (minimal/nonspecific N/V; labs often normal); Stage II 24–72 hr (RUQ pain + AST/ALT rise + INR rise + 'silent period' as patient may feel better); Stage III 72–96 hr (PEAK hepatotoxicity + coagulopathy + encephalopathy + AKI + hypoglycemia + lactic acidosis + cerebral edema); Stage IV >96 hr (recovery OR death OR transplant); MECHANISM = acetaminophen normally metabolized by glucuronidation + sulfation; small fraction via CYP2E1 → NAPQI (toxic intermediate) → conjugated by GLUTATHIONE; OVERDOSE depletes glutathione → free NAPQI causes HEPATOCYTE NECROSIS centrilobular; NAC (N-ACETYLCYSTEINE) = GLUTATHIONE PRECURSOR + direct NAPQI scavenger + improves microcirculation + antioxidant; IV PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR PROTOCOL = 150 mg/kg in 200 mL D5W loading over 60 min → 50 mg/kg in 500 mL D5W over 4 hr → 100 mg/kg in 1000 mL D5W over 16 hr = total 300 mg/kg over 21 hr; ORAL 72-hr protocol = 140 mg/kg loading → 70 mg/kg q4h × 17 doses (less preferred — vomiting + longer); CONTINUE NAC beyond 21 hr if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity (extended protocol until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending); MOST EFFECTIVE within 8–10 hr of ingestion (reduces hepatotoxicity to <5%); STILL BENEFICIAL up to 24–72 hr + even with established acute liver failure (improves outcomes per Prescott + Smilkstein); GIVE NAC BEFORE LEVEL RESULT if presentation >8 hr + high suspicion (don't wait for level); RUMACK-MATTHEW NOMOGRAM = serum acetaminophen level at ≥4 hr post-ingestion plotted on time-concentration; TREATMENT LINE at 150 μg/mL at 4 hr; treat if AT OR ABOVE line; nomogram DOES NOT APPLY to staggered + chronic + unknown-time + sustained-release + extended-release ingestion; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA for liver transplant referral in acetaminophen-induced ALF (POOR PROGNOSIS without transplant): arterial pH <7.3 after fluid resuscitation OR (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV hepatic encephalopathy); sensitivity ~70% + specificity ~90%; lactate >3.5 after fluid resuscitation OR >3.0 at 12 hr = adverse + transplant marker; UNOS STATUS 1A for acute liver failure = highest priority for transplant; PSYCHIATRIC = SUICIDE ATTEMPT requires safety + 1:1 sitter + lethal means restriction (lock up medications + remove sharps + suicide-resistant room) + 72-hr hold (CA 5150) if not voluntary + capacity assessment + multidisciplinary psychiatric consult + safety planning (Stanley-Brown brief intervention) + post-medical inpatient psychiatric care vs outpatient with safety net; COMPLICATIONS of ALF = CEREBRAL EDEMA (cause of death in 30% — ICP monitoring + mannitol + hypertonic saline + hyperventilation + therapeutic hypothermia + sedation); COAGULOPATHY (do NOT correct INR with FFP unless active bleeding or planned procedure — INR trend is prognostic + transplant criterion; vitamin K minimal effect in ALF as synthesis impaired); HYPOGLYCEMIA (glucose 10% IV); AKI (CRRT preferred over IHD for hemodynamic stability + ICP); INFECTION (broad-spectrum coverage + low threshold to treat — high infection rate + worsens outcomes); FAMILY-centered communication + ethics consult + capacity + transplant decision-making for intentional self-harm patient (treated equitably — no historical discrimination)

Patel brought to ED at 1830 via ambulance after spouse returned home at 1745 to find empty extra-strength acetaminophen 500 mg bottle (50 tablets = ~25 g) + open bottle of red wine ~1/2 bottle remaining + patient lying on couch crying + verbalizing 'I just want it all to stop.' RECOGNITION + IMMEDIATE NAC initiation are critical because effectiveness time-sensitive. Risk factors for severe hepatotoxicity not present (no chronic alcohol/CYP2E1 induction). RN role: rapid recognition + history + assessment + IV access + labs + NAC initiation + psychiatric consult + safety + family communication.

labs.n: ACETAMINOPHEN toxicity stages + mechanism + risk factors
labs.v: ACETAMINOPHEN TOXICITY 4 CLINICAL STAGES: STAGE I (0–24 hr post-ingestion) = NONSPECIFIC SYMPTOMS — nausea, vomiting, malaise, pallor, diaphoresis; LFTs often normal but already RISING by 8-24 hr in massive ingestion; patient may appear deceptively well; STAGE II (24–72 hr) = 'SILENT PERIOD' or RUQ PAIN + AST/ALT MARKED RISE (>1000 typical; can exceed 10,000 in massive ingestion) + INR rise + bilirubin rise + early renal dysfunction; patient may feel temporarily better; STAGE III (72–96 hr) = PEAK HEPATOTOXICITY + COAGULOPATHY (INR rising) + ENCEPHALOPATHY (West Haven Grade I-IV) + AKI (acute tubular necrosis + direct nephrotoxicity) + HYPOGLYCEMIA (impaired gluconeogenesis) + LACTIC ACIDOSIS + CEREBRAL EDEMA (cause of death in 30% of ALF) + sepsis + multi-organ failure; STAGE IV (>96 hr) = RECOVERY (most patients with prompt NAC) OR DEATH OR LIVER TRANSPLANT; recovery is complete with no chronic sequelae if patient survives. MECHANISM: acetaminophen normally metabolized in liver via (1) GLUCURONIDATION 55% + (2) SULFATION 35% + (3) CYP450 (mostly CYP2E1) 5–10% → NAPQI (highly toxic intermediate) → conjugated by GLUTATHIONE → harmless mercapturic acid + cysteine conjugates excreted; OVERDOSE saturates glucuronidation + sulfation → more shunted through CYP2E1 → more NAPQI; GLUTATHIONE depleted by overwhelming NAPQI → unconjugated NAPQI causes HEPATOCYTE NECROSIS (centrilobular pattern — zone 3 around central vein where CYP2E1 most active); RENAL NAPQI generation also occurs (less than liver) → AKI. TOXIC DOSE = >150 mg/kg single ingestion OR >7.5 g in adult; THERAPEUTIC max = 4 g/day in healthy adult (3 g/day in elderly + liver disease + chronic alcohol use); CHRONIC SUPRATHERAPEUTIC use (>4 g/day chronically — accidental in fasting + chronic alcohol + cytochrome inducers) can also cause hepatotoxicity at lower doses. RISK FACTORS for severe hepatotoxicity at lower doses: CHRONIC ALCOHOL USE (CYP2E1 induction + glutathione depletion); CHRONIC malnutrition + fasting (low glutathione); ISONIAZID (CYP induction); CHRONIC phenytoin + carbamazepine + rifampin (CYP induction); HIV + tuberculosis treatment (combined risks); CHRONIC use of acetaminophen + COX inhibitors (additive); pregnancy (some risk); elderly (relative). EXTENDED-RELEASE acetaminophen (Tylenol 8-hr ER) requires SECOND level at 8 hr after the first to interpret nomogram correctly. STAGGERED INGESTION over hours/days = nomogram doesn't apply — treat empirically + manage clinically.
labs.r: ACETAMINOPHEN TOXICITY proceeds through 4 stages with progressively severe hepatic + extrahepatic manifestations + risk of acute liver failure + death + transplant. MECHANISM = NAPQI toxic intermediate + glutathione depletion + centrilobular necrosis. TOXIC DOSE thresholds + RISK FACTORS (alcohol, malnutrition, drugs, age) lower the threshold. RECOGNITION + intervention time-sensitive — most effective NAC within 8-10 hr but beneficial up to 24-72 hr + ALF.

labs.v: RUMACK-MATTHEW NOMOGRAM: serum acetaminophen LEVEL drawn at ≥4 hr POST-INGESTION (drawing earlier than 4 hr may miss peak); PLOT level on time-concentration graph; TREATMENT LINE at 150 μg/mL at 4 hr — TREAT if AT OR ABOVE line at any subsequent time point (200 μg/mL at 4 hr = original 'possible toxicity' line raised to 150 in US for safety margin); UK uses 100 μg/mL line; APPLY only to: (1) SINGLE acute ingestion of known immediate-release acetaminophen with KNOWN time; (2) presentation within 24 hr (less reliable >24 hr — clinical features + LFTs guide); (3) does NOT apply to: STAGGERED ingestion over hours/days, CHRONIC supratherapeutic, UNKNOWN time of ingestion, SUSTAINED-RELEASE (need second level at 8 hr after first), EXTENDED-RELEASE, COMBINATION products with opioids/benzodiazepines/etc that affect GI motility. THIS PATIENT: level 95 μg/mL at 12 hr post-ingestion = ABOVE the 12-hr point on the treatment line (extrapolated from 4 hr = 150 μg/mL with half-life of ~4 hr → ~37 μg/mL at 12 hr; this patient's 95 is significantly above line — TOXIC + REQUIRES NAC). N-ACETYLCYSTEINE (NAC) INDICATIONS: (1) ACETAMINOPHEN level AT OR ABOVE nomogram treatment line at any time point post-ingestion; (2) UNKNOWN time of ingestion + acetaminophen detected; (3) >7.5 g ingestion estimated even if level pending; (4) STAGGERED OR CHRONIC supratherapeutic with concerning labs (AST/ALT elevation + symptoms); (5) PRESENTATION >8 hr post-ingestion + high suspicion (DON'T wait for level — give NAC empirically + obtain level after starting); (6) ALF from any cause (NAC has some benefit in non-acetaminophen ALF per Lee 2009). NAC PROTOCOLS: IV PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR (preferred in US — shorter + less vomiting + IV); LOADING 150 mg/kg in 200 mL D5W over 60 min → INFUSION 2 = 50 mg/kg in 500 mL D5W over 4 hr → INFUSION 3 = 100 mg/kg in 1000 mL D5W over 16 hr = TOTAL 300 mg/kg over 21 hr; ALTERNATIVE Acetadote pre-mixed formulation 30 mg/mL; CAN compound from oral NAC 20% solution if Acetadote unavailable. ORAL 72-HR (less preferred — vomiting + longer): 140 mg/kg PO loading → 70 mg/kg PO q4h × 17 doses. CONTINUE BEYOND 21 HR if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity ongoing: 6.25 mg/kg/hr continuous infusion until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending. NAC SIDE EFFECTS: ANAPHYLACTOID reaction in 5-15% (most with rapid loading dose) — flushing + urticaria + bronchospasm + hypotension + angioedema; manage with HOLD infusion + diphenhydramine 25-50 mg IV + epinephrine if severe + steroids + albuterol if bronchospasm + reduce rate + RESTART slower after symptoms resolve (do NOT discontinue permanently — anaphylactoid is histamine release not immunologic); nausea + vomiting common (antiemetic + ondansetron 4 mg IV q6h PRN); odor/taste unpleasant.
labs.r: RUMACK-MATTHEW NOMOGRAM = level at ≥4 hr post-ingestion + plot + treatment line at 150 μg/mL at 4 hr; APPLY only to single acute known-time ingestion + immediate-release; DOES NOT APPLY to staggered/chronic/unknown/SR/ER. NAC INDICATIONS = above nomogram + unknown time + >7.5 g + presentation >8 hr (empiric) + ALF. PRESCOTT 21-hr IV protocol preferred. Extend beyond 21 hr if hepatotoxicity ongoing. ANAPHYLACTOID reaction 5-15% — manage + restart slower. RN role: rapid level draw + plot + NAC initiation + monitoring + management of side effects.

labs.n: KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA + transplant evaluation + UNOS
labs.v: KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA for liver transplant referral in ACETAMINOPHEN-INDUCED acute liver failure (POOR PROGNOSIS WITHOUT TRANSPLANT — predicts ~80%+ mortality): EITHER (1) ARTERIAL pH <7.3 after fluid resuscitation (>24 hr post-ingestion); OR (2) ALL THREE — INR >6.5 (PT >100 sec) + Cr >3.4 mg/dL + GRADE III or IV HEPATIC ENCEPHALOPATHY (West Haven). SENSITIVITY ~70% + SPECIFICITY ~90%. LACTATE >3.5 mg/dL after fluid resuscitation OR >3.0 mg/dL at 12 hr = ADVERSE prognostic + supports transplant evaluation (added criterion in updated King's College + meta-analyses). MELD (Model for End-Stage Liver Disease) score >32 may be additional criterion. ACUTE LIVER FAILURE STUDY GROUP (ALFSG) criteria similar with refinements. NON-ACETAMINOPHEN King's College criteria are different (INR >6.5 OR 3 of 5: age <10 or >40, certain etiologies, INR >3.5, T bili >17, encephalopathy interval). UNOS STATUS 1A = HIGHEST PRIORITY for liver transplant — for ACUTE LIVER FAILURE with life expectancy without transplant <7 days; PEDIATRIC ALF + Wilson disease + hepatic artery thrombosis post-transplant + primary graft non-function also qualify; geographic + ABO + size + offer + many factors in matching; status 1A patients move ahead of all chronic liver disease patients on the list. WAIT TIME for transplant in ALF can be days to longer + many patients deteriorate before transplant available — BRIDGE therapies = supportive ICU care + plasma exchange + extracorporeal liver support (MARS, Prometheus — limited US availability); TRANSPLANT EVALUATION = comprehensive psychosocial + medical + surgical workup; for INTENTIONAL OVERDOSE/SELF-HARM cases, evaluation includes psychiatric assessment + family support + adherence likelihood — historically discrimination existed but CURRENT UNOS practice and most transplant centers TREAT EQUITABLY without discrimination based on suicide-attempt etiology (Lancet Liver editorial + transplant ethics literature); social support + psychiatric care for relapse prevention + family + caregiving plan are evaluated. POST-TRANSPLANT outcomes for acetaminophen ALF survival ~70-80% at 1 yr (similar to other ALF causes); RETURN to mental health care + outpatient psychiatry + therapy + family support central. CONTRAINDICATIONS to transplant = active sepsis without source control + active malignancy + multi-organ failure too severe to survive transplant (CV instability, cerebral edema not controllable, ARDS severe) + lack of social support precluding post-transplant adherence + active untreated substance use disorder; these are RELATIVE in many cases.
labs.r: KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA = arterial pH <7.3 OR (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy); lactate >3.5 supportive marker; UNOS Status 1A for ALF = highest priority. INTENTIONAL OVERDOSE patients treated equitably for transplant + psychiatric assessment included. Bridge therapies + comprehensive workup. Post-transplant outcomes good with appropriate aftercare.

labs.v: ACUTE LIVER FAILURE COMPLICATIONS: (1) HEPATIC ENCEPHALOPATHY — West Haven grading: Grade 0 = no abnormality; Grade I = mild confusion + euphoria + tremor + decreased attention; Grade II = lethargy + disorientation + asterixis + slurred speech; Grade III = somnolent but rousable + marked confusion + incoherent; Grade IV = coma; AMMONIA elevated (often >100; >150 = cerebral edema risk); MANAGEMENT = identify + treat precipitants (GI bleed + infection + electrolyte + medication); LACTULOSE 30-60 mL PO/NG/PR q4-6h titrated to 2-4 BMs daily (acidifies colon → NH4+ ion trapping + reduced NH3 absorption); RIFAXIMIN 550 mg PO BID (gut antibiotic reduces NH3-producing bacteria); avoid sedatives + benzodiazepines + opioids (worsen encephalopathy); intubation if Grade III-IV for airway protection; (2) CEREBRAL EDEMA — cause of death in 30% of ALF; risk increases with Grade III/IV encephalopathy + ammonia >150 + hyponatremia + ALF acute (<8 weeks); ICP MONITORING (controversial — placement risk + benefit unclear; some centers use); MANAGEMENT = HEAD OF BED 30° + AVOID neck flexion + MINIMIZE stimulation + mannitol 0.25-1 g/kg IV bolus + hypertonic saline 3% maintenance + target sodium 145-155 + therapeutic hypothermia 32-34°C (limited evidence) + HYPERVENTILATION temporarily for acute herniation (PaCO2 30-35); avoid hyperthermia + hyperglycemia + hyponatremia; (3) COAGULOPATHY — INR rises with hepatic synthetic dysfunction; DO NOT correct INR routinely with FFP unless ACTIVE BLEEDING or PLANNED PROCEDURE — INR trend is PROGNOSTIC + KING'S COLLEGE TRANSPLANT CRITERION; VITAMIN K minimal effect in ALF (synthesis impaired); FFP for bleeding or pre-procedure with INR >2 + platelets >50k for procedures; risk of bleeding + thrombosis paradoxically — DVT prophylaxis with SCDs (mechanical) while INR elevated; (4) AKI — acute tubular necrosis from direct toxicity + hypoperfusion + hepatorenal syndrome physiology in ALF; CRRT (continuous renal replacement therapy) preferred over IHD for hemodynamic stability + ICP-friendly + slower volume + solute shifts; avoid nephrotoxins (contrast + NSAIDs + aminoglycosides); maintain MAP ≥65; (5) HYPOGLYCEMIA — impaired gluconeogenesis; D10 IV continuous + hourly glucose + glucose checks; avoid hyperglycemia (worsens cerebral edema); (6) LACTIC ACIDOSIS — anaerobic metabolism + tissue hypoperfusion + impaired hepatic lactate clearance; FLUID resuscitation + vasopressors as needed + assess perfusion + treat infection; (7) INFECTION — high rate in ALF (impaired immunity + multiple lines + invasive procedures); LOW THRESHOLD to treat — broad-spectrum antibiotics + antifungals if persistent or risk factors; cultures + monitoring; surveillance cultures controversial; (8) ELECTROLYTES — hyponatremia (avoid <130), hypokalemia, hypophosphatemia (paradox — low phosphate predicts better outcome but supplement if symptomatic), hypomagnesemia; (9) RESPIRATORY — ARDS + aspiration risk + intubation for airway protection in Grade III/IV encephalopathy; (10) CARDIOVASCULAR — hyperdynamic + decreased SVR + cardiomyopathy in severe; vasopressors PRN; (11) Sepsis recognition + management.
labs.r: ACUTE LIVER FAILURE has multiple severe complications — cerebral edema is leading cause of death + requires ICP-directed management; coagulopathy NOT routinely corrected (prognostic + transplant marker); AKI managed with CRRT; hypoglycemia + lactic acidosis + infection + electrolytes + respiratory + CV all require attention. RN role: bedside surveillance + escalation + supportive care + medication management + family communication.

labs.n: PSYCHIATRIC management — 5150 hold + safety + capacity + treatment
labs.v: PSYCHIATRIC ASSESSMENT for suicide attempt: 5150 INVOLUNTARY 72-HOUR HOLD (CA Welfare & Institutions Code §5150 — danger to self/danger to others/gravely disabled; psychiatrist or psychologist or LCSW or MFT designated; involuntary detention for evaluation + treatment + decision on further care); criteria met for this patient = recent suicide attempt + ongoing assessment + need for inpatient psychiatric evaluation; LEGAL framework — patient maintains MOST rights including refusal of medication (unless emergency) + access to advocate + right to challenge hold via Riese hearing; 5250 = 14-day extension if criteria continue; 5260 = 30-day intensive treatment; conservatorship LPS for chronic. CAPACITY ASSESSMENT separate from 5150 — patient may have decisional capacity for SOME decisions (medical treatment consent in this case) while being held under 5150 for safety reasons; capacity = ability to understand + appreciate + reason + express stable choice; this patient demonstrating decisional capacity for medical decisions (consenting to NAC + understanding situation); 5150 ≠ incapacity. SUICIDE RISK ASSESSMENT components: ideation (active vs passive + intent + plan + access + lethality of method); history of attempts (1+ attempts = elevated lifetime risk; method matters); risk factors (psychiatric history + substance use + previous attempts + family hx suicide + chronic illness + recent loss + social isolation + access to lethal means); protective factors (family support + reasons for living + religious beliefs + therapy engagement + future plans); current mental status; intoxication; SAD PERSONS scale + Columbia Severity Rating Scale + ASQ + others; THIS PATIENT — recent attempt (high risk) + chronic MDD (risk factor) + relationship conflict + medication discontinuation + therapy gap; PROTECTIVE factors include children (5-yr-old twins), parents arriving, sister contact, prior treatment engagement, no chronic alcohol/substance abuse, capacity intact, no prior suicide attempts, regrets attempt verbally. SAFETY in MEDICAL SETTING during hold: 1:1 SITTER (continuous observation) for active suicide watch; LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION (room sweep — remove sharps + glass + medication carts + cords + plastic bags + restricted bathroom access; suicide-resistant fixtures if available); MEAL TRAY without utensils/glass (plastic + supervised); MEDICATION ADMINISTRATION supervised (no PRN to be self-administered or stockpiled); environmental rounds q15 min documented; family + visitor screening + supervised visits; phone use supervised; chaplain + social worker + peer support; PSYCHIATRIC liaison rounds + treatment planning; ANTIDEPRESSANT discussion — restart sertraline (was on previously) or alternative + careful + monitor for SI worsening early in treatment (black-box warning <25 yr but caution all ages early treatment); BENZODIAZEPINES generally avoided in suicidal depression (disinhibition + worsening); ANXIOLYTICS short-term limited use; CBT + DBT + family therapy + grief therapy + couples therapy + addiction therapy if comorbid; INPATIENT PSYCHIATRIC unit transfer when medically cleared for 5150 evaluation + treatment + discharge planning + outpatient linkage; CRISIS resources 988 + 741741. POST-DISCHARGE safety planning + Stanley-Brown brief intervention + means restriction (this patient — secure medications at home, lock all OTC meds in cabinet, family member to administer or supervise) + close follow-up (within 7 days post-discharge — high relapse risk in first 30 days) + outpatient psychiatrist + therapist + medication management + couples therapy + family + crisis lines.
labs.r: PSYCHIATRIC management of suicide attempt = 5150 involuntary hold + capacity assessment separate + suicide risk stratification + safety in medical setting (1:1 sitter + means restriction) + psychiatric inpatient transfer when medically cleared + discharge planning + outpatient linkage + safety planning + crisis resources. RN role: bedside safety + family + multidisciplinary + advocacy + post-medical psychiatric care.

labs.n: ETHICS + TRANSPLANT decision-making for self-harm cause
labs.v: ETHICAL CONSIDERATIONS in acetaminophen ALF from intentional overdose: (1) HISTORICAL DEBATE about transplant for self-inflicted disease — earlier transplant practice sometimes discriminated against self-harm patients; CURRENT UNOS policy + transplant center ethics + Lancet Liver consensus statements = TREAT EQUITABLY without discrimination based on suicide-attempt etiology; (2) PSYCHIATRIC EVALUATION for transplant readiness includes assessment of post-transplant adherence likelihood + family + social support + relapse risk + mental health stability + capacity for informed consent + commitment to follow-up care + medication adherence including immunosuppressants — these are reasonable transplant evaluation criteria for ANY patient; (3) PSYCHIATRIC ETHICS + PUBLIC HEALTH ETHICS reframe — depression is a TREATABLE condition + suicide attempts often impulsive + reversible + most survivors do NOT make further attempts (~9% lifetime risk re-attempt vs much higher initial concern; most patients glad they were saved per follow-up studies); (4) PATIENT CAPACITY for medical treatment decisions (this patient has decisional capacity per consult — distinct from 5150 hold which is about safety); (5) FAMILY communication + emotional support + ethics consult if conflict; (6) SHARED DECISION-MAKING with patient + family + psychiatry + hepatology + transplant + ethics + chaplain + social work + multidisciplinary; (7) DOCUMENT capacity + decision-making process + ethics consult + transplant evaluation + outcomes; (8) PSYCHIATRIC AFTERCARE essential post-transplant. CHAPLAIN + social work + ethics consult + family meetings + couples therapy + grief therapy + multidisciplinary support address ethical + emotional + relational + spiritual + meaning-making dimensions; CHILDREN (5-yr-old twins) considerations — child psychologist consult + family communication + age-appropriate disclosure with help + maintaining child-parent connection + grandparents temporarily caring; LONG-TERM RECOVERY includes individual + family + couples therapy + medication + addiction treatment if needed + ongoing mental health care.
labs.r: ETHICAL framework = transplant + ALF management EQUITABLY without discrimination for self-harm cause + psychiatric evaluation for post-transplant adherence + family + multidisciplinary + capacity + chaplain + ethics consult + shared decision-making. RN role: facilitate + advocate + document + emotional support + family communication.

labs.v: FAMILY communication for INTENTIONAL OVERDOSE / SUICIDE ATTEMPT + acute liver failure: (1) HONESTY + COMPASSION + non-judgment with family — they are experiencing acute crisis + grief + fear + guilt + ambivalence; (2) MEDICAL UPDATES regular + clear + jargon-free + answer questions + reassess; (3) TRAUMA-INFORMED approach — recognize family's emotional response + provide space + chaplain + social worker + peer support; (4) HONOR patient confidentiality + HIPAA + minor children consent issues (twins age 5 — temporarily with maternal grandparents per spouse + appropriate); information SHARING per patient consent + need-to-know basis; (5) BALANCE patient autonomy + family involvement — patient has decisional capacity + may have specific preferences about who is informed/involved; (6) MULTIDISCIPLINARY team for family — chaplain + social worker + peer support + child psychologist + couples therapy + couples therapy + family therapy + crisis lines for family + Survivors of Suicide Loss Support if applicable; (7) ANTICIPATE GRIEF if outcome unfavorable + bereavement preparation; (8) COMMUNICATION across distance — parents arriving from out of state + sister in different time zone + work colleagues + close friends + children + spouse — facilitate via phone + Zoom + in-person + written; (9) DISCHARGE planning + post-discharge support for family — crisis resources + safety planning education + means restriction at home + community resources + ongoing mental health care + couples therapy. THIS PATIENT family situation: SPOUSE R. (32, software engineer) — recently disclosed infidelity 3 weeks ago triggering crisis; estranged + considering separation BUT supportive of patient currently + at bedside despite estrangement; couples therapy × 6 months active; relationship considering reconciliation vs separation. PARENTS arriving from Phoenix — supportive + close + flying in 0600 next day. SISTER out-of-state — called pre-ingestion; aware + en route in next few days. CHILDREN twins age 5 with maternal grandparents temporarily — child psychologist consult planned + age-appropriate disclosure when patient ready + maintain child connection (visits, phone calls, video calls); CHAPLAIN visiting + family identifies as Hindu — chaplain coordinated with Hindu chaplain on staff + temple priest + family rituals supported; SOCIAL WORK case management + resources + insurance + discharge planning + community resources; CLOSE FRIENDS notified per patient consent + visiting schedule coordinated; WORK colleagues NOT notified currently per patient consent (school principal aware of medical emergency + sick leave coverage but cause not disclosed); FAMILY MEETING scheduled Day 2 with hepatology + psychiatry + ethics + social work + chaplain + patient + spouse + parents.
labs.r: FAMILY-CENTERED communication is foundational throughout this complex care. Honesty + compassion + multidisciplinary support. Patient autonomy + family involvement balance. Communication across distance. Children + spouse + parents + sister + friends. Cultural + spiritual support. Discharge planning + post-discharge support for family. RN role: facilitate + coordinate + advocate + emotional support.

labs.v: PERTINENT NEGATIVES: no salicylate co-ingestion (level 4 negative); no other co-ingestants (urine tox negative); not pregnant; no chronic alcohol use (CYP2E1 induction protective absent); no malnutrition; no isoniazid or CYP inducers; no chronic acetaminophen use; no prior suicide attempts; no other psychiatric history beyond MDD; no chronic medical comorbidities; no encephalopathy currently (Grade 0 on West Haven throughout admission); no King's College criteria met (avoided transplant pathway); no cerebral edema; no AKI severe (Cr 1.8 peak — resolved); no respiratory failure; no cardiovascular instability; no acute bleeding; no infections; ICU stay short (4 days); transferred to inpatient psych Day 4 cleared medically; recovery trajectory; family + spouse + parents engaged + supportive despite estrangement; 5-yr-old twins safe with grandparents; insurance coverage active. PROTECTIVE FACTORS: presentation within 12 hr (NAC effective + minimized hepatotoxicity); spouse found + brought to ED; capacity intact; no chronic alcohol/substance abuse; no chronic illness; supportive family despite recent conflict; reconciliation potential with spouse; therapy + medical management responsive; psychiatric inpatient care continuing; multidisciplinary team engagement; chaplain + cultural support; insurance + access to mental health care; communication + family support; children temporarily safe + age-appropriate planning; institutional protocols + 1:1 sitter + means restriction effective; post-discharge planning robust.
labs.r: Multiple PROTECTIVE FACTORS contributed to good outcome: presentation within 12 hr, capacity intact, supportive family, no chronic risk factors, multidisciplinary care, psychiatric inpatient transition. Standard expected positive trajectory with comprehensive medical + psychiatric care. Long-term outcome depends on sustained psychiatric care + therapy + relationship work + family support.

nursesNote: 1830 — 38 y/o female J. Patel admitted to ED via ambulance after spouse returned home at 1745 to find empty extra-strength acetaminophen 500 mg bottle (50 tabs = ~25 g) + open red wine + patient on couch crying + verbalizing 'I just want it all to stop.' INGESTION per patient ~1230 today (~12 hr before ED arrival; possibly staggered with wine over 2 hr). Estimated dose ~25 g acetaminophen = ~340 mg/kg in 73 kg patient (MASSIVELY toxic — threshold >150 mg/kg). PMH: MDD × 8 yr (sertraline 100 mg daily that was stopped 6 wk ago after side effects + missed PCP appointments + therapy gap × 4 mo), HTN (lisinopril controlled), migraines. SOCIAL: married 8 yr, recently strained, spouse disclosed infidelity 3 wk ago, patient considering separation; works 3rd grade teacher; 2 twins age 5 with maternal grandparents temporarily; parents flying in from Phoenix overnight; sister out-of-state. PRECIPITANT: morning argument with spouse → impulsive ingestion. NO chronic alcohol; NO other risk factors for severe hepatotoxicity. ED ASSESSMENT 1830-1900: vitals HR 102 sinus tachy + BP 108/64 + RR 18 + SpO2 97% RA + T 36.6 + GCS 15 + alert + oriented + tearful + minimizing 'it wasn't a real attempt; I just wanted to sleep'; cooperative; emesis × 3 + nausea + RUQ + epigastric pain; no jaundice; abdomen soft + mild RUQ tenderness; neuro intact + no encephalopathy. INTERVENTIONS in ED: 2 large-bore IVs (18-gauge) + arterial line + Foley + continuous monitoring + 1:1 SITTER assigned for suicide watch + LETHAL MEANS removed from area (sharps + glass + cords + medication carts away + suicide-resistant if available + bathroom door open + room sweep) + nursing every 15-min behavioral check + DOCUMENTED. LABS sent: ACETAMINOPHEN level 95 μg/mL at 12 hr post-ingestion (PLOTTED ON RUMACK-MATTHEW NOMOGRAM = ABOVE TREATMENT LINE — TOXIC + requires NAC); SALICYLATE 4 (negative); EtOH 0.08 (consistent with co-ingestion 6+ hr ago); urine tox NEGATIVE for opioids/cocaine/amphetamines/cannabis/benzodiazepines; PREGNANCY negative; AST 285 + ALT 312 (RISING — Stage II); INR 1.6 (rising from baseline 1.0); BILIRUBIN 1.2; Cr 0.9 + BUN 16 (normal renal); GLUCOSE 88; LACTATE 2.4 (mild elevation); ABG pH 7.39 + PaCO2 38 + PaO2 95 + HCO3 23 (essentially normal); AMMONIA 42 (normal); PHOSPHATE 3.2; electrolytes Na 138 + K 3.9 + Cl 100 + Mg 1.9 + iCa 4.6; CBC Hgb 13.8 + plt 220 + WBC 11.2. TOXICOLOGY + PSYCHIATRY + HEPATOLOGY consulted. NAC INITIATED 1945 — IV PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR PROTOCOL: LOADING 150 mg/kg = 11,000 mg in 200 mL D5W over 60 min via dedicated infusion pump (Acetadote 30 mg/mL premixed) → completed 2045 WITHOUT anaphylactoid reaction (5-15% incidence — flushing/urticaria/bronchospasm/hypotension; manage with HOLD + diphenhydramine + epi if severe + steroids + albuterol + reduce rate + restart slower); INFUSION 2 = 50 mg/kg = 3650 mg in 500 mL D5W over 4 hr started 2050 ends 0050; INFUSION 3 = 100 mg/kg = 7300 mg in 1000 mL D5W over 16 hr starts 0050 ends 1650 next day; total 300 mg/kg over 21 hr; antiemetic ondansetron 4 mg IV given for ongoing nausea; will CONTINUE NAC beyond 21 hr if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity (extended 6.25 mg/kg/hr continuous until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending). PSYCHIATRY CONSULT 1930 completed by psychiatry resident + attending: 5150 INVOLUNTARY 72-HOUR HOLD initiated (CA WIC §5150 — danger to self per recent suicide attempt + ongoing risk assessment + need for inpatient psychiatric evaluation); patient has DECISIONAL CAPACITY for medical treatment decisions (separate from 5150 hold for safety) — alert + oriented + understanding of situation + appropriate communication + consents to NAC + comprehends gravity; SUICIDE RISK STRATIFICATION = HIGH (recent attempt + ongoing risk) but mitigated by multiple PROTECTIVE FACTORS (children, parents support, no chronic substance use, capacity intact, accepting treatment, regret verbalized, family engaged); psychiatric inpatient transfer planned when medically cleared (anticipated 24-72 hr based on NAC + ALF surveillance); SAFETY in medical ICU = 1:1 sitter + lethal means restriction + meal tray without utensils/glass + supervised medication administration + every 15-min documented behavioral checks + supervised visitors + supervised phone use + chaplain + social worker + family liaison; sertraline restart deferred to psychiatric inpatient unit + careful monitoring early treatment; benzodiazepines avoided. FAMILY engagement: spouse R. (estranged but currently supportive + at bedside; couples therapy ongoing × 6 mo); parents flying in from Phoenix arriving 0600 next day; sister out-of-state en route in coming days; close friends notified per patient consent; work colleagues NOT disclosed (school principal aware of medical emergency only); children (5-yr-old twins) safe with maternal grandparents temporarily — child psychologist consult planned for age-appropriate disclosure when patient ready; chaplain visit + Hindu chaplain coordination + family rituals respected; social work case management + insurance + community resources + discharge planning. ETHICS consult activated for transplant decision-making preparation (if needed) + intentional self-harm cause (treated equitably per current ethics + UNOS — no discrimination based on suicide-attempt etiology; psychiatric evaluation for post-transplant adherence + family + adherence likelihood + multidisciplinary). Transferred to ICU Bed 5 at 2100: single-room + continuous monitoring + 1:1 sitter + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION documented + chaplain + social worker + family liaison ongoing. PLAN: complete 21-hr NAC protocol + EXTEND beyond if hepatotoxicity ongoing (INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000); LABS q4h ATM + acetaminophen level downtrending + trend AST/ALT + INR + Cr + glucose + lactate + ammonia + ABG; HEPATOLOGY surveillance + transplant center notified for early listing if criteria met; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA monitoring (arterial pH <7.3 OR INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy); UNOS Status 1A would apply if criteria met; West Haven encephalopathy grading q4h; if encephalopathy Grade III/IV → intubation for airway + cerebral edema management (HOB 30° + mannitol + hypertonic saline + therapeutic hypothermia consideration + avoid sedation worsening); HYPOGLYCEMIA monitor + D10 IV if persistent + hourly glucose; INFECTION low threshold to treat with broad-spectrum if signs; AKI surveillance + CRRT if needed (preserves hemodynamic + ICP); coagulopathy NOT corrected with FFP unless active bleeding (INR is transplant criterion + prognostic — don't mask); DVT prophylaxis mechanical SCDs (avoid heparin while INR elevated); psychiatric inpatient transfer when medically cleared + 5150 ongoing + discharge planning + outpatient psychiatry + therapy + couples therapy + Stanley-Brown safety plan + means restriction at home (lock all medications + family supervision + community resources + crisis lines 988 + 741741 + close follow-up within 7 days post-discharge); multidisciplinary family meetings ongoing; chaplain + ethics + social work continued. Hospital Day 4 morning update: AST 1200 + ALT 1850 (downtrending from peak 6800/7200), INR 1.6 (downtrending from 4.8), Cr 1.2 (resolving from 1.8), lactate 1.8 (normalized), ammonia 48 (improving), no encephalopathy throughout; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA never met (avoided transplant pathway); HEPATIC RECOVERY trajectory; MEDICALLY CLEARED for inpatient psych transfer Day 4 evening for 5150 evaluation + treatment + discharge planning + outpatient linkage + safety planning + family work; recovery positive but VIGILANT for re-attempt risk (highest first 30 days post-discharge).
providerOrders: ADMIT to Medicine ICU Bed 5 (single-room + continuous monitoring + 1:1 sitter for suicide watch + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION protocol active)

DIAGNOSIS: intentional acetaminophen overdose ~25 g (~340 mg/kg) ~12 hr post-ingestion with co-ingested alcohol; Stage I-II acetaminophen toxicity with rising AST/ALT/INR; major depressive disorder; suicide attempt; 5150 involuntary 72-hr hold for safety
CODE STATUS: FULL CODE; advance directives not on file; healthcare proxy = spouse (currently estranged but supportive)
SAFETY + 1:1 SITTER + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION:
Continuous 1:1 sitter for suicide watch

Lethal means restriction: sweep room — remove sharps, glass, cords, medication carts, plastic bags, restricted bathroom access if needed; suicide-resistant fixtures preferred; meal trays without utensils/glass (plastic + supervised); supervised medication administration; nothing for PRN self-administration; restricted visitor access with screening
Behavioral check every 15 min documented
Supervised visits + phone use
No PRN medications that could be diverted
Psychiatric inpatient transfer when medically cleared
ACETAMINOPHEN TOXICITY MANAGEMENT — N-ACETYLCYSTEINE (NAC) IV PROTOCOL:
PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR IV regimen (HIGH-ALERT med with double-check + dedicated infusion pump):
LOADING DOSE: 150 mg/kg = 11,000 mg in 200 mL D5W over 60 minutes (Acetadote 30 mg/mL premixed)
INFUSION 2: 50 mg/kg = 3650 mg in 500 mL D5W over 4 hr
INFUSION 3: 100 mg/kg = 7300 mg in 1000 mL D5W over 16 hr
TOTAL = 300 mg/kg over 21 hr
EXTEND beyond 21 hr if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity ongoing: 6.25 mg/kg/hr continuous infusion until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending

ANAPHYLACTOID REACTION watch (5-15% incidence with rapid loading — flushing + urticaria + bronchospasm + hypotension + angioedema): HOLD infusion + diphenhydramine 25-50 mg IV + epinephrine if severe + steroids + albuterol if bronchospasm + reduce rate + RESTART slower
Ondansetron 4 mg IV q6h PRN for nausea (NAC + acetaminophen toxicity emesis)
Pre-mixed Acetadote 30 mg/mL preferred; compound from oral NAC 20% if Acetadote unavailable
LABS + MONITORING:
Acetaminophen level at admission + q4h × 24 hr then q12h until cleared
AST + ALT + INR + total + direct bilirubin + GGT + alk phos q4h × 48 hr then q6-12h
Cr + BUN + electrolytes (Na + K + Cl + Mg + Phos + iCa) q6h
Glucose q1-2h (hypoglycemia risk in Stage III — D10 IV if needed)
Lactate q4h
Ammonia q12h (rising precedes encephalopathy)
ABG (arterial pH for King's College monitoring) q12h or with clinical change
CBC daily
PT/PTT/fibrinogen daily + with clinical change
Salicylate level (rule out co-ingestion) — done in ED, negative
Urine drug screen — done in ED, negative for other substances
Pregnancy test — done in ED, negative
EtOH level — done in ED, 0.08 consistent with co-ingestion
Lipase + amylase (rule out pancreatitis) if abdominal pain worsens
Cultures (blood + urine + sputum) if infection signs
KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA + TRANSPLANT REFERRAL monitoring:
Hepatology consult immediate + bedside + ongoing
Transplant center notified for surveillance + early listing if criteria met
CRITERIA: arterial pH <7.3 after fluid resuscitation OR (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV hepatic encephalopathy)
Lactate >3.5 mg/dL after fluid resuscitation or >3.0 at 12 hr = adverse + supports listing
UNOS Status 1A = highest priority if criteria met
ETHICS consult for transplant + intentional self-harm cause (treated equitably per UNOS + current practice)
Psychiatric evaluation for post-transplant adherence + family + support + relapse risk + multidisciplinary
HEPATIC ENCEPHALOPATHY surveillance + management:
West Haven grading q4h (0 = no abnormality; I = mild confusion/euphoria; II = lethargy/asterixis; III = somnolent/incoherent; IV = coma)
Cerebral edema = leading cause of death in ALF (30%)

IF Grade III/IV → intubation + airway protection + cerebral edema management (HOB 30° + avoid neck flexion + minimize stimulation + mannitol 0.25-1 g/kg IV PRN + hypertonic saline 3% maintenance + target Na 145-155 + therapeutic hypothermia 32-34°C consideration + hyperventilation temporarily for herniation PaCO2 30-35); avoid hyperthermia + hyperglycemia + hyponatremia
Lactulose 30-60 mL PO/NG/PR q4-6h titrated to 2-4 BMs daily (acidifies colon → NH4+ trapping)
Rifaximin 550 mg PO BID (gut antibiotic for ammonia-producing bacteria)
AVOID sedatives + benzodiazepines + opioids (worsen encephalopathy + mask exam)
OTHER COMPLICATIONS management:

Coagulopathy: DO NOT routinely correct INR with FFP (INR is prognostic + transplant criterion); vitamin K 10 mg IV daily (minimal effect in ALF but try); FFP only if active bleeding or planned procedure with INR >2
AKI: maintain MAP ≥65; CRRT (continuous renal replacement therapy) preferred over IHD if needed (hemodynamic + ICP-friendly); avoid nephrotoxins
Hypoglycemia: D10 IV continuous if persistent + hourly glucose + glucose checks; D50 IV bolus for severe; AVOID hyperglycemia (worsens cerebral edema)
Lactic acidosis: fluid resuscitation + vasopressors PRN + assess perfusion + treat infection
Infection: LOW THRESHOLD to treat with broad-spectrum antibiotics + antifungals + cultures + surveillance
Electrolytes: avoid hyponatremia (<130) + replace hypokalemia + hypophosphatemia + hypomagnesemia
DVT prophylaxis: mechanical SCDs (avoid heparin while INR elevated)
PSYCHIATRIC MANAGEMENT:
Psychiatry consult immediate + bedside + ongoing
5150 involuntary 72-hr hold initiated (CA WIC §5150)
Suicide risk assessment ongoing + safety planning + Stanley-Brown brief intervention
Capacity assessment for medical decisions (intact currently — separate from 5150 safety hold)
Transfer to inpatient psychiatric unit when medically cleared

ANTIDEPRESSANT: deferred to psychiatric inpatient unit for restart + monitoring + careful early treatment + black-box warning informed consent; restart sertraline (previously on) or alternative; AVOID rapid restart in acute medical setting
AVOID benzodiazepines (worsen depression + disinhibition + suicide risk); short-term anxiolytic only if needed for severe agitation in psych setting
Therapy: CBT + DBT + family therapy + grief + couples therapy + outpatient linkage
Crisis resources: 988 Suicide + Crisis Lifeline; Crisis Text Line text HOME to 741741; mobile crisis; ER
Means restriction at home post-discharge: lock all medications + family supervision + remove sharps if any concern + community resources
Close outpatient follow-up within 7 days post-discharge (high re-attempt risk first 30 days)
FAMILY + multidisciplinary engagement:
Hepatology + Toxicology + Psychiatry + Transplant + Ethics + ICU intensivist coordinated
Social Work case management + insurance + community resources + discharge planning
Chaplain (Hindu) + family rituals respected
Family meetings Day 2 + Day 4 + as needed: hepatology + psychiatry + ethics + social work + chaplain + patient + spouse + parents
Child psychologist for age-appropriate disclosure for 5-yr-old twins (temporarily with maternal grandparents)
Spouse + parents + sister communication + supportive presence (despite estrangement)
Couples therapy continuation post-medical management
Workplace coordination (school principal aware of medical leave; cause not disclosed per patient consent)
Insurance enrollment + benefits + disability if needed
HIPAA confidentiality + patient consent for information sharing + family + close contacts
DOCUMENTATION:
Toxicology + history + ingestion details + dose + time + co-ingestants + risk factors
Labs trending + Rumack-Matthew plotting + King's College criteria assessment
NAC infusion documentation + side effects + double-check
Psychiatry assessment + 5150 paperwork + capacity assessment + risk stratification + safety plan
Family communication + multidisciplinary updates + ethics consult notes
Sensitive content in mental health section of EHR + privacy considerations + appropriate access control
Discharge planning + outpatient linkage + safety plan + crisis resources
Patient + family education + teach-back + understanding confirmation

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters at this ED admission?
steps.inst: Select ALL findings that are clinically + psychiatrically significant cues requiring nursing attention.

opts.text: INTENTIONAL ACETAMINOPHEN OVERDOSE ~25 g (~340 mg/kg in 73 kg patient — MASSIVELY toxic; threshold >150 mg/kg) ~12 hr post-ingestion with co-ingested wine (alcohol potentiates initial absorption but chronic alcohol — which patient does NOT have — is the CYP2E1 induction risk factor); estimated dose + time + co-ingestants all critical for NAC + nomogram + prognosis
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — recognition of overdose severity + time-sensitive management. Massive dose at 340 mg/kg + 12 hr presentation = NAC required + still effective (most effective within 8-10 hr but beneficial up to 24-72 hr + even with ALF). Recognition triggers urgent management cascade.

opts.text: ACETAMINOPHEN LEVEL 95 μg/mL at 12 hr post-ingestion = ABOVE Rumack-Matthew treatment line (extrapolated from 150 μg/mL at 4 hr) → REQUIRES NAC; level at ≥4 hr post-ingestion is gold standard; nomogram APPLIES to single acute known-time immediate-release ingestion only — does NOT apply to staggered/chronic/unknown time/sustained-release/extended-release
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — Rumack-Matthew nomogram + indication for NAC. Level above line = NAC required. Nomogram limitations recognized. Level at 4+ hr critical timing. RN role: lab interpretation + nomogram plotting + NAC initiation.

opts.text: N-ACETYLCYSTEINE IV PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR PROTOCOL: LOADING 150 mg/kg over 60 min → 50 mg/kg over 4 hr → 100 mg/kg over 16 hr = total 300 mg/kg over 21 hr; CONTINUE BEYOND 21 hr if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity ongoing (extended protocol 6.25 mg/kg/hr until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending); ANAPHYLACTOID reaction 5-15% — manage with HOLD + diphenhydramine + epi if severe + steroids + albuterol + reduce rate + restart slower
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — NAC administration + side effect management. 21-hr IV protocol preferred. Extended infusion if hepatotoxicity ongoing. Anaphylactoid management (not immunologic — restart at slower rate). RN role: administration + monitoring + management + escalation.

opts.text: STAGES of acetaminophen toxicity: I 0-24 hr (nonspecific + N/V — patient currently here); II 24-72 hr (RUQ + AST/ALT rising — patient entering); III 72-96 hr (PEAK hepatotoxicity + coagulopathy + encephalopathy + AKI + hypoglycemia + lactic acidosis + cerebral edema); IV >96 hr (recovery OR death OR transplant); LABS trending q4h critical
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — toxicity stages guide monitoring + intervention. Stage III is highest-risk window for cerebral edema + transplant criteria. RN role: anticipate progression + serial labs + clinical surveillance + early escalation.

opts.text: KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA for liver transplant referral in acetaminophen-induced ALF (POOR prognosis without transplant): arterial pH <7.3 after fluid resuscitation OR (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV hepatic encephalopathy); lactate >3.5 after fluid resuscitation or >3.0 at 12 hr = adverse marker; UNOS Status 1A = highest priority for ALF; transplant center notified for surveillance + early listing
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — King's College criteria + transplant pathway. Predicts ~80%+ mortality without transplant. UNOS Status 1A highest priority. Surveillance + early listing critical. RN role: monitor labs + clinical features + escalate if criteria met.

opts.text: PSYCHIATRIC ASSESSMENT: 5150 INVOLUNTARY 72-HR HOLD (CA WIC §5150 — danger to self per recent suicide attempt); SAFETY in medical setting = 1:1 SITTER for suicide watch + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION (room sweep + no sharps + glass + cords + meal tray supervised + every 15-min behavioral check + supervised visitors + phone) + meds supervised + chaplain + social worker + family liaison; capacity assessment SEPARATE from 5150 hold; capacity intact for medical decisions in this patient
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — psychiatric safety in medical setting. 5150 hold + 1:1 sitter + means restriction + multidisciplinary support. Capacity assessment separate from 5150 (capacity for treatment decisions vs safety hold). RN role: safety + advocacy + multidisciplinary + family + documentation.

opts.text: FAMILY-CENTERED communication: spouse (estranged after infidelity disclosure but supportive); parents flying in from Phoenix; sister out-of-state; 5-yr-old twins safe with maternal grandparents; child psychologist consult planned; chaplain (Hindu) family rituals supported; couples therapy continuing; social work case management + insurance + community resources; multidisciplinary family meetings
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — family-centered care + multidisciplinary support. Complex family situation. Patient consent for information sharing + HIPAA. Children + spouse + parents + sister + friends. Cultural + spiritual support. Discharge planning.

opts.text: Allergies: PCN (rash 5 yr ago — never reproduced); document and proceed; no other allergies; no medication contraindications in current regimen
opts.rat: Allergy history documentation. PCN rash documented + non-anaphylactic. No current medication contraindications. Document and proceed. Not the priority cue but important for safety.

opts.text: WAIT to administer NAC until acetaminophen level result is available + nomogram interpretation is complete + ABG confirms acidosis + comprehensive imaging is obtained
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach for this patient. PRESENTATION >8 hr post-ingestion + high-dose suspected ingestion = GIVE NAC EMPIRICALLY + obtain level after starting (NAC effectiveness diminishes with each hour delay; most effective within 8-10 hr but beneficial up to 24-72 hr + even with established ALF). Don't wait for level result if presentation late + high suspicion. NAC has wide safety margin + low risk profile + treating empirically + early is much safer than waiting + missing window.

opts.text: Discharge the patient from the ED to her spouse's care once she is medically stable + NAC infusion complete + she promises to follow up with her outpatient therapist + her depressive symptoms are likely temporary post-conflict
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + DANGEROUS. SUICIDE ATTEMPT requires inpatient evaluation + treatment + 5150 hold for safety + lethal means restriction + multidisciplinary care + transition to inpatient psychiatric unit + comprehensive discharge planning + outpatient linkage + close follow-up within 7 days (HIGHEST relapse risk first 30 days). DISCHARGE TO HOME with promise + therapist follow-up alone is INADEQUATE + creates serious re-attempt risk. SUICIDE ATTEMPT IS NEVER a 'temporary post-conflict' issue — it requires comprehensive psychiatric care.

opts.text: CORRECT the rising INR with FFP 4 units to normalize coagulation + reduce bleeding risk + improve patient comfort
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. DO NOT routinely correct INR in acute liver failure — INR is PROGNOSTIC + KING'S COLLEGE TRANSPLANT CRITERION (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy = transplant indication). FFP correction MASKS the trend + makes prognostic assessment + transplant eligibility evaluation impossible. FFP RESERVED FOR ACTIVE BLEEDING or PLANNED INVASIVE PROCEDURE. Vitamin K 10 mg IV daily has minimal effect in ALF (synthesis impaired) but try. DVT prophylaxis mechanical SCDs only (avoid heparin while INR elevated).

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What toxicology + pharmacology + ethics principles apply?
steps.inst: Select ALL accurate linkages between cues and underlying principles.

opts.text: ACETAMINOPHEN MECHANISM: normally metabolized 90% glucuronidation + sulfation; 5-10% CYP2E1 → NAPQI (toxic) → glutathione conjugation → harmless; OVERDOSE saturates glucuronidation + sulfation + depletes glutathione → free NAPQI causes CENTRILOBULAR HEPATOCYTE NECROSIS; NAC = glutathione precursor + direct NAPQI scavenger + antioxidant + improves microcirculation
opts.rat: CORRECT — acetaminophen mechanism + NAC mechanism. Centrilobular zone 3 necrosis. NAC pharmacology multimodal. Foundation for treatment understanding.

opts.text: TOXIC DOSE >150 mg/kg single ingestion OR >7.5 g in adult (LOWER threshold with chronic alcohol + malnutrition + fasting + isoniazid + CYP inducers); 4 STAGES of toxicity — I 0-24 hr nonspecific; II 24-72 hr RUQ + AST/ALT rise; III 72-96 hr peak hepatotoxicity + complications; IV >96 hr recovery or death or transplant; recognition + intervention time-sensitive
opts.rat: CORRECT — toxic dose + stages framework. Risk factors lower threshold. Stage progression predictable. Foundation for monitoring + intervention.

opts.text: RUMACK-MATTHEW NOMOGRAM: serum level at ≥4 hr post-ingestion plotted on time-concentration; treatment line at 150 μg/mL at 4 hr; treat if AT OR ABOVE line at any time point; APPLY only to single acute known-time immediate-release; DOES NOT APPLY to staggered/chronic/unknown/sustained-release/extended-release — treat empirically with NAC + monitor clinically
opts.rat: CORRECT — nomogram framework + limitations. Single acute known-time + IR only. Empiric treatment for other scenarios. Foundation for NAC decision.

opts.text: NAC IV PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR PROTOCOL: 150 mg/kg over 60 min → 50 mg/kg over 4 hr → 100 mg/kg over 16 hr = 300 mg/kg total; EXTEND if hepatotoxicity ongoing (INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000) with 6.25 mg/kg/hr until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending; ANAPHYLACTOID 5-15% manage with HOLD + diphenhydramine + epi if severe + reduce rate + restart slower; ORAL 72-hr protocol alternative (less preferred)
opts.rat: CORRECT — NAC IV protocol. Standard 21-hr regimen + extension criteria + anaphylactoid management. Foundation for treatment.

opts.text: KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA for liver transplant in acetaminophen ALF: arterial pH <7.3 after fluid resuscitation OR (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy); lactate >3.5 supportive; UNOS Status 1A highest priority; INTENTIONAL OVERDOSE patients treated equitably per current ethics + UNOS without discrimination; psychiatric evaluation for adherence + family + multidisciplinary
opts.rat: CORRECT — King's College criteria + transplant ethics. Equitable treatment regardless of self-harm cause. Psychiatric evaluation. UNOS Status 1A. RN role: monitor + advocate + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: ALF COMPLICATIONS: cerebral edema (cause of death 30% — ICP management + mannitol + hypertonic + therapeutic hypothermia); coagulopathy (do NOT routinely correct — prognostic + transplant criterion); AKI (CRRT preferred — hemodynamic + ICP-friendly); hypoglycemia (D10 IV); lactic acidosis; infection (low threshold); electrolytes; respiratory; CV; SEPSIS recognition
opts.rat: CORRECT — ALF complications framework. Multiple severe complications. Cerebral edema leading cause of death. Coagulopathy management nuanced. Foundation for ICU care.

opts.text: PSYCHIATRIC management: 5150 INVOLUNTARY 72-HR HOLD (CA WIC §5150 — danger to self + 14-day 5250 if continued); CAPACITY ASSESSMENT separate from 5150 (capacity = ability to understand + appreciate + reason + express); SUICIDE RISK STRATIFICATION (recent attempt + protective + risk factors); SAFETY in medical setting = 1:1 sitter + lethal means restriction + multidisciplinary + chaplain + social worker + family liaison; inpatient psychiatric transfer when medically cleared; AVOID benzodiazepines in suicidal depression
opts.rat: CORRECT — psychiatric management framework. 5150 + capacity + risk stratification + safety + multidisciplinary + inpatient transfer + outpatient linkage. RN role: safety + advocacy + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: WAIT for acetaminophen level + ABG + comprehensive imaging before initiating NAC — treatment without confirmation could expose patient to unnecessary side effects
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. NAC is TIME-SENSITIVE + most effective within 8-10 hr but beneficial up to 24-72 hr + ALF; presentation >8 hr post-ingestion + high suspicion = GIVE NAC EMPIRICALLY + obtain level after starting (don't wait). NAC has wide safety margin + low risk profile. Each hour of delay reduces effectiveness. Waiting risks irreversible hepatotoxicity + ALF + transplant + death. SAFER to treat early + adjust based on results than wait + miss window.

opts.text: DISCHARGE patient from ED to spouse's care once medically stable + promises outpatient follow-up — depression is temporary + suicide attempt was impulsive + low ongoing risk
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS. SUICIDE ATTEMPT requires inpatient evaluation + safety + multidisciplinary + means restriction at home + close outpatient follow-up + crisis resources + safety planning. RE-ATTEMPT RISK HIGHEST in first 30 days post-discharge. DISCHARGE TO HOME without comprehensive care plan = unsafe + creates relapse risk. Patient needs inpatient psychiatric evaluation + treatment + careful discharge planning.

opts.text: Patients who attempt suicide should be EXCLUDED from liver transplant evaluation because they are at high risk of re-attempt + transplant resource is limited + should be reserved for patients with no self-harm causes
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG ethical position. CURRENT UNOS POLICY + transplant center ethics + Lancet Liver consensus + AAHPM/AAOS ethics literature = TREAT EQUITABLY without discrimination based on self-harm etiology. Psychiatric evaluation for post-transplant adherence + family support + relapse risk = reasonable transplant evaluation criteria for ANY patient (not exclusion based on cause). Depression is TREATABLE + most suicide attempt survivors do NOT re-attempt + most are GLAD they were saved per follow-up studies. Exclusion creates additional harm + violates equitable care principles.

opts.text: ROUTINELY correct INR with FFP to optimize hemostasis in all ALF patients regardless of bleeding status
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. DO NOT routinely correct INR in ALF — INR is PROGNOSTIC + KING'S COLLEGE TRANSPLANT CRITERION (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy = transplant indication). FFP correction MASKS the trend + makes prognostic assessment + transplant eligibility impossible. FFP RESERVED FOR ACTIVE BLEEDING or PLANNED INVASIVE PROCEDURE. Vitamin K 10 mg IV daily minimal effect (synthesis impaired). DVT prophylaxis mechanical SCDs only.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Rank nursing concerns at this ED admission
steps.inst: Rank these nursing concerns from #1 (HIGHEST priority) to #5 (LOWEST).

opts.text: Acetaminophen Toxicity + NAC Initiation r/t intentional overdose ~25 g (~340 mg/kg) ~12 hr ago + level 95 μg/mL above Rumack-Matthew treatment line + AST/ALT/INR rising; IV NAC Prescott/Smilkstein 21-hr protocol (150 → 50 → 100 mg/kg) + extend if hepatotoxicity ongoing; anaphylactoid surveillance + management
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. Time-sensitive toxicology emergency. NAC effectiveness diminishes hourly. Recognition + immediate initiation prevents/limits hepatotoxicity + ALF + transplant + death. Foundation for all subsequent care.

opts.text: Suicide Safety + 5150 Hold + Means Restriction r/t intentional overdose + ongoing risk; 5150 involuntary 72-hr hold; 1:1 sitter + lethal means restriction in medical setting (sharps + glass + cords + meds + visitors supervised); 15-min behavioral checks; psychiatric consult + multidisciplinary support; inpatient psychiatric transfer when medically cleared
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Immediate patient safety + suicide prevention in medical setting. Safety protocols + means restriction + multidisciplinary engagement. RN role: continuous bedside safety + advocacy + escalation.

opts.text: Acute Liver Failure Surveillance + King's College Criteria r/t Stage II progressing toward III; q4h labs (AST/ALT/INR/Cr/glucose/lactate/ammonia/ABG); hepatology + transplant center surveillance; UNOS Status 1A if criteria met (arterial pH <7.3 OR INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy); cerebral edema + coagulopathy + AKI + hypoglycemia + lactic acidosis surveillance + management
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. ALF surveillance + transplant eligibility monitoring + complication management. Hepatology + transplant + ICU coordination. RN role: serial monitoring + clinical surveillance + escalation.

opts.text: Multidisciplinary Coordination + Capacity Assessment + Ethics r/t hepatology + toxicology + psychiatry + transplant + ethics + ICU intensivist coordinated; capacity assessment for medical decisions (intact currently — separate from 5150 safety hold); ethics consult for transplant decision-making + intentional self-harm cause (treated equitably); chaplain + social worker + family liaison
opts.rat: #4 — Significant + ongoing. Multidisciplinary coordination essential for complex case. Capacity assessment respects autonomy. Ethics consult for difficult decisions. Foundation for ongoing care + decision-making.

opts.text: Family Communication + Discharge Planning + Outpatient Linkage r/t spouse (estranged but supportive) + parents from Phoenix + sister + children (5-yr-old twins with grandparents) + family meetings + child psychologist + couples therapy + chaplain + social worker; outpatient psychiatry + therapy + means restriction at home + crisis resources + close follow-up within 7 days post-discharge
opts.rat: #5 — Important throughout + at discharge. Family-centered care + comprehensive discharge planning + outpatient transition. Multidisciplinary discharge. Foundation for long-term recovery + safety.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH intervention, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this patient.

opts.text: IV N-ACETYLCYSTEINE PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR PROTOCOL: 150 mg/kg in 200 mL D5W over 60 min loading → 50 mg/kg in 500 mL D5W over 4 hr → 100 mg/kg in 1000 mL D5W over 16 hr = 300 mg/kg total; EXTEND beyond 21 hr if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity (6.25 mg/kg/hr until INR <2); anaphylactoid surveillance + management with diphenhydramine + epi + steroid + reduce rate + restart slower
opts.rat: Standard NAC protocol for acetaminophen toxicity. Time-sensitive. Extend if hepatotoxicity ongoing. Anaphylactoid management. RN role: administer + monitor + manage.

opts.text: 1:1 SITTER + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION + 5150 INVOLUNTARY 72-HR HOLD (CA WIC §5150 — danger to self); room sweep (sharps + glass + cords + meds + cart away); meal tray without utensils/glass; supervised medication administration + visitors + phone; 15-min behavioral checks; psychiatric inpatient transfer when medically cleared
opts.rat: Standard safety in medical setting for suicide attempt. 5150 protocol + sitter + means restriction + 15-min checks + multidisciplinary engagement. RN role: continuous bedside safety + documentation.

opts.text: Q4H LABS — acetaminophen level, AST + ALT + INR + total + direct bilirubin + GGT + alk phos; Cr + BUN + electrolytes (Na/K/Cl/Mg/Phos/iCa); GLUCOSE q1-2h (hypoglycemia risk Stage III); LACTATE q4h; AMMONIA q12h; ABG q12h or with clinical change; CBC daily; PT/PTT/fibrinogen daily; cultures if infection signs

opts.text: WEST HAVEN ENCEPHALOPATHY GRADING q4h; LACTULOSE 30-60 mL PO/NG/PR q4-6h titrated to 2-4 BMs daily; RIFAXIMIN 550 mg PO BID; AVOID sedatives + benzodiazepines + opioids; INTUBATION if Grade III/IV for airway protection + cerebral edema management (HOB 30° + mannitol + hypertonic saline + therapeutic hypothermia 32-34°C consideration)

opts.text: HEPATOLOGY + TRANSPLANT center surveillance; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA monitoring (arterial pH <7.3 OR INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV encephalopathy); UNOS Status 1A if criteria met; ETHICS consult for transplant + intentional self-harm cause (treated equitably); psychiatric evaluation for post-transplant adherence + family

opts.rat: Comprehensive family-centered care + multidisciplinary support. Spouse + parents + children + chaplain + social work + couples therapy. Discharge planning + outpatient linkage. Foundation for long-term recovery.

opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. NAC is TIME-SENSITIVE + give EMPIRICALLY if presentation >8 hr + high suspicion + obtain level after starting. NAC effectiveness diminishes hourly. Each hour of delay reduces effectiveness. Empirical treatment safer than waiting + missing window. Standard practice for late presentations.

opts.text: DISCHARGE patient from ED to spouse's care once medically stable + NAC infusion complete + promises outpatient therapist follow-up — depression is temporary + suicide attempt was impulsive + low ongoing risk + outpatient management is sufficient
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS. RE-ATTEMPT RISK HIGHEST first 30 days. Inpatient psychiatric care + careful discharge planning + outpatient linkage essential. DISCHARGE to home with promise alone is INADEQUATE.

opts.text: EXCLUDE patient from liver transplant evaluation because suicide attempt is the cause of her acute liver failure + transplants should be reserved for non-self-harm causes
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG ETHICAL position. CURRENT UNOS POLICY + transplant center ethics = TREAT EQUITABLY without discrimination based on self-harm etiology. Depression is TREATABLE + most suicide attempt survivors do NOT re-attempt + most are GLAD they were saved per follow-up studies. Psychiatric evaluation for post-transplant adherence + family + relapse risk = reasonable transplant evaluation criteria for ANY patient (not exclusion). Excluding violates equitable care + creates additional harm.

opts.text: ROUTINELY CORRECT INR with FFP 4 units to optimize hemostasis + reduce bleeding risk; vitamin K 10 mg IV daily; DVT prophylaxis with enoxaparin 40 mg SQ daily
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. DO NOT routinely correct INR in ALF — INR is PROGNOSTIC + KING'S COLLEGE TRANSPLANT CRITERION. FFP correction MASKS the trend + makes prognostic assessment + transplant eligibility impossible. FFP RESERVED FOR ACTIVE BLEEDING or PLANNED INVASIVE PROCEDURE. Vitamin K 10 mg IV daily minimal effect (synthesis impaired) but try. DVT prophylaxis MECHANICAL SCDs only (avoid heparin while INR elevated).

opts.text: Administer LORAZEPAM 1 mg IV PRN q4h for anxiety + insomnia in this patient + restart SERTRALINE 100 mg PO daily today for chronic depression
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. AVOID benzodiazepines in suicidal depression (disinhibition + worsening + sedation interferes with encephalopathy exam + masks hepatic encephalopathy emergence). Short-term anxiolytic only if absolutely necessary for severe agitation in psych setting after medical clearance. SERTRALINE restart DEFERRED to psychiatric inpatient unit (careful early treatment + monitoring + black-box warning informed consent + crisis plan); not initiated in acute medical setting + acute liver failure (hepatic metabolism + uncertain pharmacokinetics in ALF).

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence at ED admission
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: ESTABLISH 2 large-bore IVs (16 or 18-gauge) + arterial line + Foley + continuous monitoring; SEND acetaminophen level + salicylate + EtOH + urine tox + pregnancy + AST/ALT/INR/bili/Cr/BUN/electrolytes/glucose/lactate/ammonia/ABG/CBC/coags; INITIATE NAC EMPIRICALLY (don't wait for level — 12 hr post-ingestion + high dose suspicion) per Prescott/Smilkstein 21-hr IV protocol
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Time-sensitive NAC initiation + IV access + comprehensive labs. Empiric NAC for late presentation. Foundation for all subsequent care. RN role: rapid + coordinated.

opts.text: ACTIVATE PSYCHIATRY consult + 5150 INVOLUNTARY 72-HR HOLD initiated (CA WIC §5150 — danger to self); 1:1 SITTER assigned + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION protocol activated (room sweep + suicide-resistant + 15-min checks + supervised meds/visitors/phone); SAFETY first; capacity assessment for medical decisions separate from 5150 (intact in this patient)

opts.text: HEPATOLOGY + TOXICOLOGY + TRANSPLANT center notification + ETHICS consult; King's College criteria monitoring + UNOS Status 1A surveillance + transplant eligibility evaluation (equitable for self-harm cause); psychiatric eval for post-transplant adherence + family + multidisciplinary
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Multidisciplinary engagement + transplant pathway preparation. Hepatology + transplant + ethics + psychiatric. RN role: facilitate + coordinate.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each finding, classify the trajectory.

opts.text: Hospital Day 1 morning 0700 (24 hr post-ingestion): NAC infusion 3 of 21-hr protocol 50% complete; acetaminophen 30 μg/mL (downtrending); AST 1850 + ALT 2100 (rising — Stage II); INR 2.4 (rising); Cr 1.0 (stable); glucose 78 (mild decrease — monitor); lactate 3.2; ammonia 78; phosphate 2.6; ABG pH 7.36; King's College criteria not met currently; trending continues; NAC will be extended beyond 21 hr given evolving hepatotoxicity
opts.rat: Stage II hepatotoxicity progression expected. NAC continuing + extension planned. King's College criteria monitoring. Worsening labs reflects expected toxin effect + recovery phase upcoming. RN role: continued monitoring + escalation + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: Hospital Day 2 morning 0700 (48 hr post-ingestion): NAC continued at 6.25 mg/kg/hr extended protocol; acetaminophen <10 μg/mL (cleared); AST 6800 + ALT 7200 (PEAK hepatotoxicity); INR 4.8 (rising); Cr 1.8 (mild AKI); glucose 68 (hypoglycemia — D10 IV initiated); lactate 4.6 (rising); ammonia 124; ABG pH 7.32 (acidosis); King's College criteria not yet met (no encephalopathy + arterial pH not yet <7.3) but APPROACHING; hepatology + transplant center re-engaged + UNOS 1A consideration
opts.rat: Stage III peak hepatotoxicity. Multiple complications emerging (AKI + hypoglycemia + lactic acidosis + ammonia rising + acidosis approaching). Transplant evaluation accelerating. King's College criteria approached but not met. RN role: intensive monitoring + escalation + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: Hospital Day 3 morning: AST 4500 + ALT 5200 (downtrending from peak — recovery beginning); INR 3.2 (downtrending); Cr 1.4 (resolving); glucose 88 (D10 weaned); lactate 2.8 (improving); ammonia 78 (improving); ABG pH 7.38 (normalizing); NAC continues; no encephalopathy throughout; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA NEVER MET (avoided transplant pathway); hepatic recovery trajectory; psychiatric consult Day 3 + 5150 hold continues + inpatient transfer planning
opts.rat: Recovery phase. Stage IV transition. Hepatic function recovering. King's College criteria never met. NAC discontinuation criteria approaching. Psychiatric transfer planning. Standard expected recovery trajectory.

opts.text: Hospital Day 4 morning: AST 1200 + ALT 1850 (downtrending from peak 6800/7200); INR 1.6 (downtrending from 4.8); Cr 1.2 (resolving); glucose 92 (stable + diet); lactate 1.8 (normalized); ammonia 48 (improving); ABG pH 7.42 (normalized); NAC discontinued Day 3 evening; MEDICALLY CLEARED for inpatient psychiatric transfer Day 4 evening; 5150 evaluation + treatment + discharge planning + outpatient linkage + family work + safety planning

opts.text: Hospital Day 2 evening — patient develops Grade III hepatic encephalopathy (somnolent + confused + asterixis) + ammonia 180 + ABG pH 7.28 + INR 7.2 + Cr 3.8; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA NOW MET (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III encephalopathy); intubated for airway protection + cerebral edema management (HOB 30° + mannitol + hypertonic saline + avoid hyperthermia + therapeutic hypothermia 32-34°C consideration); UNOS STATUS 1A listing activated; family meeting + ethics consult + transplant evaluation accelerated
opts.rat: ACUTE LIVER FAILURE with cerebral edema + King's College criteria met = transplant pathway activated + UNOS Status 1A listing + intubation + intensive ICU care + family meetings + ethics. Severe but expected progression in ALF + appropriate escalation. RN role: immediate recognition + escalation + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: Hospital Day 5 — inpatient psychiatric unit Day 1: 5150 evaluation + treatment + safety planning ongoing; SSRI restart (sertraline 50 mg daily) initiated + black-box warning informed consent + careful monitoring + frequent follow-up; CBT/DBT individual therapy + group therapy + family therapy + couples therapy + medication management + psychiatric education + discharge planning; family + spouse + parents + sister supportive; insurance + community resources
opts.rat: Standard inpatient psychiatric trajectory + treatment initiation + multidisciplinary + family engagement. SSRI restart with monitoring. Discharge planning. Foundation for sustained recovery.

opts.rat: Standard discharge with comprehensive plan + outpatient linkage + safety planning + family + couples + children. Foundation for sustained recovery. Multidisciplinary coordination achieved goals. Standard expected positive trajectory.

opts.text: 3-month follow-up: patient stable on sertraline 100 mg + outpatient therapy + couples therapy progressing + reconciliation with spouse + reunited family + working part-time + children well + parents + sister regular contact + crisis-free; ongoing therapy + medication + community support + meaning-making + gratitude expressed; no re-attempt + receptive to ongoing care + chaplain visits supportive; long-term recovery trajectory positive
opts.rat: Excellent 3-month outcome — sustained recovery + comprehensive care + family healing + work + community + meaning-making. Demonstrates value of comprehensive medical + psychiatric + family + multidisciplinary care for suicide attempt patients. Standard ideal trajectory.

opts.text: RN Day 1 administers FFP 4 units to correct INR 2.4 + vitamin K 10 mg IV + enoxaparin 40 mg SQ daily for DVT prophylaxis; INR drops to 1.4 within 6 hr; HEPATOLOGY identifies INR masking + transplant evaluation compromised + clinical safety concerns; medication safety review + protocol education + just culture
opts.rat: ⚠️ FFP CORRECTION INAPPROPRIATE in ALF — masks INR trend + prevents King's College criteria monitoring + transplant eligibility evaluation. Enoxaparin inappropriate while INR elevated (bleeding risk). Standard medication safety + protocol education + just culture response. INR is PROGNOSTIC + transplant criterion — DO NOT correct routinely. FFP only for active bleeding or pre-procedure. SCDs for DVT prophylaxis.

Demonstrates that suicide attempt survivors can recover meaningfully with appropriate care + most are glad they were saved per follow-up studies. Standard ideal long-term trajectory. Equitable care + comprehensive treatment + family + community + meaning-making achieve recovery.
